rationales.hedonism: He stayed "an extra forty minutes" helping a colleague and got home after Brigitte had already eaten, showing pleasure and ease were sacrificed to work and duty.
rationales.achievement: The department "scored highest on the new pedagogical alignment rubric," and he savors that external confirmation of long-built competence.
rationales.benevolence: He "stayed an extra forty minutes" to help Katja handle a parent complaint, investing real time in supporting a colleague.

rationales.achievement: He is preoccupied with not being nominated and checks the spreadsheet to see if he's "slipping," showing strong concern with performance and recognition.
rationales.power: The sting comes from status allocation: he fixates on who was "put... forward" and on being overlooked despite having "been building this department" for years.

rationales.achievement: He admits he "hadn't touched my own conference paper all week" and keeps yielding the time he meant to protect for his own professional goal.
rationales.benevolence: He agrees to review Leon's rubric and help Simone with a difficult family, with his evenings repeatedly going to "other people's problems."
He forgot to call his sister on her birthday and could tell she noticed he was "going through the motions," showing a close relationship being neglected.

rationales.hedonism: He "managed the whole afternoon without opening the laptop" and enjoyed a trail walk with Brigitte that felt like "coming back to something."
rationales.benevolence: He spends intentional time with Brigitte, sharing memories and openly discussing the conference paper and his competing obligations.

rationales.achievement: He spends the evening on Leon's rubric "instead of the conference paper again," explicitly recognizing a pattern of neglecting his own goal.
rationales.benevolence: He uses his evening to review Leon's work, continuing to prioritize helping a colleague over his own deadline.

rationales.achievement: He requested to attend the conference and was told he was "long overdue for professional development," a clear step toward growth and competence.
rationales.benevolence: He called Hilde "properly this time" and listened with full attention, and she noticed he sounded "more present."

rationales.achievement: He writes "two decent pages" of the paper and treats getting the argument into shape as a meaningful accomplishment.
rationales.universalism: His paper argues that democratic participation should be taught as a practiced civic skill, showing concern with broader social functioning beyond his immediate circle.

rationales.self_direction: He tells the group he needs to "scale back for the next two weeks" and discovers that the feared backlash existed mostly in his own head.
rationales.achievement: After setting that boundary, he returns to the draft, gets "another page done," and the paper's structure starts to take shape.
He admits that whenever he chooses between being present and something with a "measurable outcome," he picks the latter and values what he can later point to and prove.
rationales.benevolence: Brigitte has stopped inviting friends because he cancels or sits "half-present," and he recognizes that both she and his sister have adjusted to his absence.

rationales.achievement: He is willing to drive back to Nashik midweek because "the deal isn't done," showing strong task focus and persistence.
rationales.security: He returns quickly because a supplier renegotiation and a coming client audit threaten operational stability, and he prioritizes containing that risk.

rationales.achievement: He frames his life story through building the company into "a proper operation" and weighs a capital decision in terms of solid numbers and timing.
rationales.tradition: He expected the business to be "something for the next generation to step into," reflecting a strong generational continuity mindset around family enterprise.
He goes to Maa's unexpectedly, eats dal made "the way Baba used to like it," and is grounded by the same table and steel plates from childhood.
rationales.benevolence: He seeks out his mother late at night and is comforted by quietly being cared for when she "just set an extra plate."

rationales.achievement: He compares his firm's scale and recognition to Rajiv's and defends his own record with concrete performance markers like employment and delivery reliability.
rationales.power: The newspaper profile and the thought that nobody writes about his company show an active sensitivity to prestige, visibility, and standing.
rationales.tradition: In the response he measures himself against his father's standard of quality and enduring customer trust rather than sheer size, explicitly invoking inherited business values.

rationales.hedonism: He wants the stillness of Varanasi and family time but refuses the full week off because work obligations take precedence over rest and enjoyment.
rationales.achievement: He centers the need to personally handle the biodegradable-line decision and candidate interviews, treating business advancement as non-delegable.
rationales.security: He justifies staying back because the investment is a major commitment and "some things you sign yourself," emphasizing control and stability.
rationales.tradition: He is drawn to the family gathering, the ghats, and the aarti, describing Varanasi as a place with a distinctive "stillness" that matters to him.

rationales.achievement: He signs off on the investment after reviewing the projections twice and says making the decision felt good, showing satisfaction in decisive business progress.
rationales.tradition: He defends his stance by citing that his father "worked six days a week until he was seventy-two," using an inherited model of family and work duty.

rationales.achievement: He is energized by the trial process and likes the moment when something "decided on paper becomes real in your hands," showing strong engagement with making the new line work.
rationales.tradition: Aarav keeping Maa's brass diya and Patil's tie to their fathers both matter to him as signs of continuity that should be carried forward.

rationales.achievement: He reorganizes meetings, delegates supplier follow-up, and sets a concrete target for the third trial and mid-April spec, keeping the launch moving.
rationales.tradition: He is deeply moved that Aarav found a temple abroad on his own, reading it as proof that "something underneath all of that" was passed down.
rationales.benevolence: He arrives a day earlier for Varanasi because it matters to Shobha and calls Aarav simply because he "wanted to hear his voice."

rationales.hedonism: He says they "ate too much," let the kids run around them, and slept better than he had "in months," clearly embracing rest and enjoyment.
rationales.tradition: The trip centers on family gathering and the aarti at the ghat, grounding him in ritual and inherited social roles.
rationales.benevolence: He values being "just someone's son-in-law, someone's uncle" for a few days, finding relief in relational belonging rather than constant work demands.

rationales.achievement: He takes the board seat while the product line is launching and celebrates that the fourth trial finally "hit target spec," showing strong commitment to advancing work and competence.
rationales.tradition: He accepts the board role partly because "his father and mine started in the same market forty years ago," treating continuity and inherited ties as a reason to serve.
rationales.benevolence: He frames the board seat as a way to help "the next group of small manufacturers," gives his team a day off, and shares the milestone with Aarav.
